noteP1: We picked fifteen companies from our portfolio that map to the priorities of Humana's information and technology organization: frontier AI and the infrastructure to run it economically, the data foundations underneath, and the security and developer tooling that let both move to production.
noteP2: Each card opens to a brief and the angle we think fits Humana best, and you can filter by category. Our full portfolio of 150+ active companies is linked in the top right. Whenever it's convenient, send back the ones worth a closer look.
portfolioIntro: Every company here works at the infrastructure layer, across AI, data, security, and developer tooling, with an angle on where it fits Humana's agenda. Filter by category, then expand any card for the brief.

anthropic.pull: Humana is modernizing cloud and data systems while rolling out human-centered agentic AI for member service and standards-based health-data interoperability. That makes the model layer consequential: it must reason over benefit documents, claims, provider records, care plans, call transcripts, eligibility data and clinical information while remaining governable enough for PHI, benefit accuracy, human accountability, fairness, CMS compliance and uptime. Anthropic matters because Claude combines long-context reasoning, agent capabilities and Claude Code with an explicit emphasis on steerability and predictable enterprise behavior. It could help member advocates, clinicians, care managers, provider teams, engineers and compliance leaders work through complex material, give engineering teams a common assistant across member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications, and support agents without turning the model into the final authority. The fit is strongest where Humana needs simpler member and provider experiences with more timely, connected health information.
cases: Reason across benefit documents, claims, provider records, care plans, call transcripts, eligibility data and clinical information without forcing teams into a separate knowledge system.
Use Claude Code across member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications and the integration services behind them.
Keep human review, model policy and audit evidence explicit in high-consequence workflows.

wispr.pull: For Humana, the fastest route to better operational data may be improving how people enter it. Wispr Flow sits beneath existing software as a voice-first input layer, allowing member advocates, clinicians, care managers, provider teams, engineers and compliance leaders to dictate member calls, care-management conversations, provider outreach, clinical notes and service reviews into the systems already in use. Spoken observations become usable text at the speed of the work, including on mobile. That would reduce documentation friction across member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications, preserve more of the rationale behind decisions and create a higher-quality record for analytics, handoffs and agentic workflows.
cases: Turn member calls, care-management conversations, provider outreach, clinical notes and service reviews into structured entries while the context is still fresh.
Work inside member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications rather than creating another application to manage.
Create a richer decision trail for handoffs, analytics and supervised agents.

browserbase.pull: For Humana, browser automation is a practical way to connect modern agents with provider portals, CMS systems, pharmacy sites, partner tools and legacy healthcare web applications before every dependency is rebuilt or exposed through an API. Browserbase provides the secure, programmable sessions, scaling and observability needed to make that connection production-ready. Agents could complete narrow steps across question-to-answer, claim-to-care and data-to-decision workflows, while engineering teams use the same infrastructure to validate member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications under realistic conditions. The strategic value is less manual web work without accepting an unmanaged collection of scripts, stored credentials and opaque browser sessions.
cases: Automate bounded tasks across provider portals, CMS systems, pharmacy sites, partner tools and legacy healthcare web applications without waiting for every system to expose an API.
Run repeatable browser tests across member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications before customer- or employee-facing releases.
Centralize sessions, credentials and execution logs for governed agent activity.

coder.pull: Developer productivity at Humana is constrained when setup, hardware and local configuration differ across the teams building member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications. Coder replaces that variability with centrally configured cloud development environments that can be provisioned from policy and shut down when not in use. The model is relevant to PHI, benefit accuracy, human accountability, fairness, CMS compliance and uptime because code, credentials and regulated tools remain inside the enterprise boundary. It also gives new engineers and acquired or distributed teams a consistent starting point, shortening the path from access request to a productive, compliant workspace.
cases: Standardize cloud workspaces for teams building member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications.
Keep source code, credentials and approved AI tooling inside controlled infrastructure.
Reduce onboarding and environment drift across distributed, acquired or specialized teams.

token.pull: As Humana adds agents to question-to-answer, claim-to-care and data-to-decision workflows, every useful workflow introduces a non-human principal with credentials, permissions and an owner—or an ownership gap. Token Security matters because it treats those identities as a distinct governance domain rather than an extension of employee access. The platform can continuously find service accounts, FHIR APIs, contact-center bots, integration credentials and AI agents, govern their lifecycle and enforce access based on intended purpose. That would help Humana scale automation while keeping the blast radius around member, claims, provider, clinical, call-center, pharmacy and quality data narrow and the audit trail clear.
cases: Inventory service accounts, FHIR APIs, contact-center bots, integration credentials and AI agents and tie every credential to an accountable owner.
Constrain agent access to the specific member, claims, provider, clinical, call-center, pharmacy and quality data required for each workflow.
Preserve lifecycle and entitlement evidence for audits, regulators and internal risk review.

fal.pull: Humana has practical multimodal needs across member education, care-navigation content, training video and multimodal health information. fal matters because it gives product and engineering teams a high-speed inference layer for image, video, audio and voice models through simple APIs, without forcing each use case to become an infrastructure project. The information and technology organization could use it to prototype and scale bounded generative experiences alongside member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications, from content production to simulation or visual assistance. The strongest fit is where latency and iteration speed matter but the risk profile around PHI, benefit accuracy, human accountability, fairness, CMS compliance and uptime still requires approved models, controlled inputs and a clear review path.
cases: Embed image, video, audio or voice generation into new member, provider, care, interoperability and agent-assist capabilities.
Prototype against multiple media models through one developer-facing inference layer.
Keep review, consent and brand or safety controls in the surrounding application workflow.

montecarlo.pull: Humana's data platform has to support its current agenda—modernizing cloud and data systems while rolling out human-centered agentic AI for member service and standards-based health-data interoperability—and remain trusted continuously, not only after a dashboard or agent produces a bad answer. Monte Carlo can monitor member, claims, provider, clinical, call-center, pharmacy and quality data from ingestion through transformation, model and agent behavior, connecting a failure to the systems and teams affected. That gives member advocates, clinicians, care managers, provider teams, engineers and compliance leaders a faster way to isolate incidents and prevent recurrence. For the information and technology organization, the value is a single trust plane across traditional analytics and emerging agentic workflows, reducing the gap between data quality, model reliability and business impact.
cases: Monitor member, claims, provider, clinical, call-center, pharmacy and quality data from source through dashboards, models and agent actions.
Trace incidents to the affected member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications and accountable engineering teams.
Prevent silent data failures from undermining simpler member and provider experiences with more timely, connected health information.

tonic.pull: Humana's current agenda—modernizing cloud and data systems while rolling out human-centered agentic AI for member service and standards-based health-data interoperability—makes safe access to representative data a bottleneck for software and AI teams. Tonic is designed for that bottleneck. It can replace production copies of member, claims, clinical, provider, pharmacy and call-center data with synthetic or de-identified datasets suitable for development, testing, demonstrations and model evaluation. This gives teams more freedom to iterate across member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications while reducing the number of environments that contain sensitive records. It also makes privacy controls easier to explain because protection is built into the dataset, not left entirely to downstream access policy.
cases: Replace production copies of member, claims, clinical, provider, pharmacy and call-center data in development and test environments.
Generate rare and edge-case scenarios for member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications and AI evaluation.
Reduce privacy exposure while giving teams faster access to useful data.

netbox.pull: Humana depends on cloud platforms, contact centers, care sites, partner networks and corporate infrastructure, but automation is only as reliable as the inventory and configuration data behind it. NetBox Labs matters as a source of truth for documenting, managing and automating IT and network infrastructure. It could give the information and technology organization one current model of sites, devices, circuits, addresses and dependencies across that estate. That foundation makes changes to Google Cloud-centered modernization alongside healthcare APIs and legacy core systems safer, improves incident context and gives infrastructure agents a trusted state to read before they act. It replaces stale spreadsheets with governed infrastructure context and supports simpler member and provider experiences with more timely, connected health information.
cases: Create a maintained source of truth across cloud platforms, contact centers, care sites, partner networks and corporate infrastructure.
Drive provisioning and change validation for Google Cloud-centered modernization alongside healthcare APIs and legacy core systems from intended state.
Ground infrastructure automation and agents in accurate topology and ownership data.

valar.pull: Humana is moving member assist, care coordination, interoperability, claims operations and engineering agents toward production, where throughput, cost and data boundaries become architecture decisions rather than model-selection details. Valar matters because it runs high-volume async, batch and agentic inference inside the customer's own VPC. The platform can optimize scheduling, batching, routing and accelerator placement while prompts, outputs, logs, models and policies remain inside Humana's environment. That is especially relevant to PHI, benefit accuracy, human accountability, fairness, CMS compliance and uptime: the information and technology organization can increase usable AI capacity without creating a separate external data path for sensitive workloads.
cases: Run member assist, care coordination, interoperability, claims operations and engineering agents inside Humana's own VPC and security boundary.
Increase usable accelerator capacity through scheduling, batching and routing.
Keep prompts, outputs, logs, models and policies under enterprise control.

echo.pull: Software supply-chain risk at Humana begins with what applications and agents are built on. Echo provides CVE-free, maintained alternatives for container bases, libraries, VMs, Helm charts and end-of-life components, removing known vulnerability work at the source. Applied to member, claims, care, provider, contact-center and data-platform applications, it would let engineering and security teams spend less time patching the same underlying package across many products. That supports simpler member and provider experiences with more timely, connected health information while strengthening the evidence around HIPAA, CMS rules, Medicare controls, interoperability, model governance and consumer protection, particularly for workloads that need hardened or FIPS-aligned components.
cases: Standardize hardened dependencies across member, claims, care, provider, contact-center and data-platform applications.
Remove known vulnerabilities before they enter application and agent build pipelines.
Use FIPS-validated options where stronger assurance is required.

act.pull: The cloud-security question for Humana is not only who has access; it is which application, service or agent can communicate with which other system. Act Security is built to answer that question continuously. By learning intended and observed pathways across Google Cloud-centered modernization alongside healthcare APIs and legacy core systems, the platform can identify unnecessary reachability and help teams lock it down with business context intact. That supports simpler member and provider experiences with more timely, connected health information by reducing lateral movement and configuration ambiguity while preserving the connections its people and production workflows genuinely need.
cases: Map intended and actual communication across Google Cloud-centered modernization alongside healthcare APIs and legacy core systems.
Create least-privilege perimeters around member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications and their data services.
Reduce lateral-movement risk without deploying another host agent.

clover.pull: As Humana expands member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications, security decisions need to move upstream into the planning of new member, provider, care, interoperability and agent-assist capabilities. Clover Security matters because it brings product-security context into the tools where requirements and designs are already being discussed, using sources such as Jira, Confluence and Drive to identify risk before implementation. That would help member advocates, clinicians, care managers, provider teams, engineers and compliance leaders receive concrete security guidance while changes are still inexpensive to make. The result is a design process that treats PHI, benefit accuracy, human accountability, fairness, CMS compliance and uptime as an input to product work rather than a late release gate.
cases: Review new member, provider, care, interoperability and agent-assist capabilities while requirements and architecture are still being shaped.
Map security decisions back to tickets, design documents and accountable owners.
Reduce late-stage rework across member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications without adding another approval queue.

drata.pull: Humana has to prove trust across HIPAA, CMS rules, Medicare controls, interoperability, model governance and consumer protection while its technology estate and third-party relationships continue to change. Drata matters because it turns controls, evidence, risks and assurance signals into a continuously monitored trust graph rather than a periodic collection exercise. The information and technology organization could use it to connect policies and owners to the systems behind member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications, surface control drift and reuse evidence across overlapping reviews. That makes compliance more operational and gives leadership a current view of the risks that could slow progress toward simpler member and provider experiences with more timely, connected health information.
cases: Continuously map controls and evidence across member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications and Google Cloud-centered modernization alongside healthcare APIs and legacy core systems.
Reuse assurance work across HIPAA, CMS rules, Medicare controls, interoperability, model governance and consumer protection instead of rebuilding it for every review.
Give leaders a current view of control drift, ownership and third-party risk.

orca.pull: Humana's cloud estate spans Google Cloud-centered modernization alongside healthcare APIs and legacy core systems, increasing the number of workloads, identities, data stores and AI services that security teams must understand together. Orca Security matters as an agentless CNAPP that unifies posture, workload, data, vulnerability and compliance context across AWS, Azure and GCP. It could give the information and technology organization immediate coverage without another deployment campaign, then prioritize the attack paths that place member, claims, provider, clinical, call-center, pharmacy and quality data or member, claims, provider, care, contact-center, interoperability and digital-health applications at real risk. That context is critical to simpler member and provider experiences with more timely, connected health information because cloud scale makes raw alert volume an ineffective operating model.
cases: Gain agentless visibility across Humana's cloud estate and emerging AI workloads.
Prioritize attack paths into sensitive Humana data and critical applications.
Unify posture, workload, data, vulnerability and compliance remediation in one view.
